title: ammophila arenaria subsp. arundinacea
intro: Ammophila arenaria subsp. arundinacea, also known as Marram Grass, is a coastal plant species that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Marram Grass include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Marram Grass typically involve infusions or teas made from the plant's leaves and stems, and can be consumed as a natural remedy for various ailments.
